codeReview.summary: Both frontend handoff packets (operations and commercial) are well-structured, thorough, and accurately grounded in the codebase. Route tables, RBAC matrices, payload families, read/write boundaries, and cross-module seam descriptions were verified against actual controller code, DTOs, and PortalRoleActionMatrix constants. The documentation is discoverable from docs/INDEX.md with clear descriptions. All cross-referenced module docs and flow docs exist. Minor simplifications in payload descriptions (omitting non-essential fields) are acceptable for a handoff-oriented document.

issues.description: SalesOrderRequest payload description omits `totalAmount`, `currency`, and `gstInclusive` fields that exist in the actual DTO. While these are secondary fields for a frontend handoff summary, `totalAmount` is @NotNull and might be important for frontend developers to know about. Consider noting that additional required fields exist.

InventoryAdjustmentRequest payload description omits `adjustmentAccountId` (which is @NotNull in the DTO) and `adminOverride`. The `adjustmentAccountId` is a required field that frontend developers would need to supply.

CreditLimitOverrideRequestCreateRequest is described as needing `packagingSlipId` or `salesOrderId` but the actual DTO also accepts `dealerId`, `overrideAmount`, and `reason`. The description says `overrideAmount` and `reason` which matches, but `dealerId` is also present in the record and not mentioned.

Section 5.1 Read/Write Boundaries says 'SALES/FACTORY can update but limited fields' for brand update, but the CatalogController's updateBrand method has no narrower role restriction (it's at class-level ADMIN, ACCOUNTING, SALES, FACTORY). The handoff RBAC summary table (Section 3.1) correctly shows ACCOUNTING has 'Full (read) / Brand-item (write)' but the boundary note is slightly misleading — the code doesn't enforce field-level restrictions at the controller level for SALES/FACTORY on brand update.

nonBlockingIssues.description: SalesOrderRequest omits required `totalAmount` field and other fields (`currency`, `gstInclusive`) that exist in the actual DTO. Frontend developers may miss a required field.
InventoryAdjustmentRequest omits required `adjustmentAccountId` field and `adminOverride`. The adjustmentAccountId is @NotNull in the DTO.

nonBlockingIssues.location: Section 5.1 boundary note for brand update
nonBlockingIssues.description: Claims SALES/FACTORY have 'limited fields' for brand update, but controller-level RBAC doesn't enforce field-level restrictions — the note may be aspirational or derive from service-layer validation not visible at the controller level.

summary: PASS. Both frontend handoff packets (operations and commercial) are comprehensive, well-organized, and accurately grounded in the codebase. All route tables were cross-checked against actual controllers and match. RBAC roles were verified against @PreAuthorize annotations and PortalRoleActionMatrix constants — all accurate. Payload families are described at a useful summary level with minor omissions of non-obvious required fields (totalAmount in SalesOrderRequest, adjustmentAccountId in InventoryAdjustmentRequest). Cross-references to all 6 module docs and 3 flow docs were verified to exist. INDEX.md correctly links to both new packets plus the pre-existing accounting portal handoff. The documentation is discoverable, truthful, and useful for frontend developers. Three non-blocking issues noted around payload field completeness and one boundary-note accuracy concern — none block the feature.
